expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: context intake preserves component-stack constraints, embedding lifecycle assumptions, and integration-sensitive customer expectations
expectations.evidence: The result explicitly states the case focus and maps it into Phase 1 intake requirements across all three areas: “Focus: context intake preserves (1) component-stack constraints, (2) embedding lifecycle assumptions, (3) integration-sensitive customer expectations.” It then has dedicated sections “1) Component-stack constraints”, “2) Embedding lifecycle assumptions”, and “3) Integration-sensitive customer expectations” describing what Phase 1 must preserve.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase1
expectations.evidence: The output is framed as a Phase 1 contract check and discusses Phase 1-specific artifacts/validation: “Primary phase under test: Phase 1 (context intake via spawn-manifest generation)”, “Output `phase1_spawn_manifest.json`”, and Phase 1 “--post” validation requirements. It concludes Phase 1 compliance is “Not directly verifiable… (artifact missing)”, which is still Phase 1-aligned evaluation.
